label: Mild eczema flare without infection or systemic symptoms
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of mild eczema or atopic dermatitis flare.
No secondary infection, severe/widespread flare, systemic illness, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, eye/genital involvement, immunocompromised host, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Cellulitis, abscess, pus, fever, secondary infection, severe pain, or rapidly spreading rash.
Skin peeling, blistering, mucosal lesions, eye/genital involvement, severe/widespread flare, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology plan.
Medication selection not supplied by clinician.
must_not_miss: Stevens-Johnson syndrome or toxic epidermal necrolysis.
Anaphylaxis or severe allergic reaction mimic.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care or dermatology if symptoms are not improving, recur often, or require ongoing medicine adjustment.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports eczema/atopic-dermatitis flare framing, moisturizer and trigger-avoidance care, prescribed medication adherence, and escalation for infection signs, severe/widespread rash, or systemic symptoms. Autonomous Phase 1651-1700 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed mild eczema flare without secondary infection, severe/widespread flare, mucosal/eye/genital involvement, severe drug-rash concern, systemic illness, pregnancy/pediatric/immunocompromised pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports eczema/atopic-dermatitis flare framing, moisturizer and trigger-avoidance care, prescribed medication adherence, and escalation for infection signs, severe/widespread rash, or systemic symptoms. Autonomous Phase 1651-1700 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed mild eczema flare without secondary infection, severe/widespread flare, mucosal/eye/genital involvement, severe drug-rash concern, systemic illness, pregnancy/pediatric/immunocompromised pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of mild eczema or atopic dermatitis flare
no infection, fever, systemic symptoms, mucosal/eye/genital involvement, or severe cutaneous adverse reaction documented
no high-risk host, pregnancy, pediatric, severe/widespread, or specialist-directed pathway
